meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A technical case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: To implement robust SaaS pricing without prior testing knowledge, leverage AI-driven test generation. Use Claude Code to scaffold your Stripe integration and Antigravity to generate edge-case unit tests. By focusing on automated validation, you ensure your billing logic remains secure and reliable, transforming your project into a production-ready portfolio piece.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a new SaaS project is exhilarating, but the specter of 'no testing knowledge' often turns that excitement into paralysis. You've built the features, but the fear of a billing bug breaking your production environment keeps you from shipping. It is the invisible wall between a student project and a professional SaaS.

Today, we shatter that wall. We aren't just hacking together a payment script; we are engineering a resilient pricing architecture. Using the combined power of Antigravity and Claude Code, we will move from uncertainty to mastery, ensuring your pricing logic is as robust as your code.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Establish your tiers and features in a structured format before writing code to ensure logic consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a tiered SaaS pricing model (Basic, Pro, Enterprise) including feature flags and monthly/yearly billing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file pricing_schema.json --content 'Define the JSON structure for a 3-tier SaaS subscription model'.

steps.title: Scaffold Stripe Integration
steps.description: Generate the backend boilerplate for Stripe checkout sessions using Claude Code's context-aware generation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure Node.js endpoint for creating a Stripe checkout session based on the previously defined pricing schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a Stripe checkout session handler in index.js using the stripe-node library.'

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Use Antigravity to write comprehensive tests for your pricing logic without needing deep manual testing expertise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest unit tests for the pricing logic, covering edge cases like currency conversion, discount code application, and subscription cancellation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test suite in pricing.test.js that validates the subscription logic using Jest.'

steps.title: Simulate Webhook Events
steps.description: Verify your system reacts correctly to asynchronous Stripe events like payment failures or subscription upgrades.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a mock webhook handler test that simulates a 'customer.subscription.deleted' event to verify system state updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create an integration test for the webhook listener to handle Stripe event payloads.'

steps.title: Final Security Audit
steps.description: Perform a final review of your pricing implementation to ensure no sensitive logic is exposed to the client-side.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current codebase for security vulnerabilities, specifically looking for exposed API keys or client-side pricing manipulation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Perform a security audit on the billing module and recommend hardening steps.'

pro_tips: Always use environment variables for Stripe API keys; never hardcode them in your repository.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode in your pricing logic to test calculations without actual charge events.
Use Antigravity to write 'negative tests'—intentionally pass invalid data to ensure your system fails gracefully.
Document your pricing schema in a README.md to demonstrate architectural thinking to future employers.
Monitor your logs in real-time during development to catch silent failures in the Stripe webhook handshake.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing logic is tested and secure, deploy your app to a staging environment and perform a live test payment with Stripe's test mode keys.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to build professional-grade SaaS pricing without needing years of QA experience. Stop fearing bugs and start shipping features that generate revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
